answer:
We use a likelihood ratio test for VAR lag length restrictions. The test statistic is  
\[
(T - c)(\log|\Sigma_4| - \log|\Sigma_6|)
\]  
where \(T = 250\), \(c = 6 \times 3 + 1 = 19\) (or sometimes just \(6 \times 3 = 18\) depending on the constant treatment; here using \(c = 6 \times 3 = 18\) is common).  
\(\log(0.0049) \approx -5.318\), \(\log(0.0036) \approx -5.626\), difference \(\approx 0.308\).  
With \(T - c = 250 - 18 = 232\), the statistic is \(232 \times 0.308 \approx 71.5\), but with \(c = 19\) it is \(231 \times 0.308 \approx 71.1\).
